channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features a variety of third-party integrations, allowing developers to connect their AI agents with tools like GitHub and Notion. This enhancement aims to facilitate the creation of practical applications using AI.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 will take place on May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This event aims to engage attendees and highlight advancements in AI technology.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving related to agentic AI. The event promises specialized tracks covering various advanced topics, enhancing developers' skills.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which enhances GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Google Cloud's Wednesday Build Hour is a weekly session designed to help developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on building. The program focuses on advanced topics, providing practical results for participants.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to streamline coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to enhance collaboration between developers and AI.

item.title: Introducing Finish Changes and Outlines, now available in Gemini Code Assist extensions on IntelliJ and VS Code
item.description: Google's Gemini Code Assist now includes features like Finish Changes and Outlines in IntelliJ and VS Code, designed to reduce developer friction. These tools help automate coding tasks and improve code comprehension.

item.title: Two different attackers poisoned popular open source tools - and showed us the future of supply chain compromise
item.description: Recent supply chain attacks have compromised popular open source tools, leading to significant data breaches. These incidents highlight the vulnerabilities within software supply chains and the need for better security measures.

item.title: Your Push Notifications Aren’t Safe From the FBI
item.description: The FBI has raised concerns about the security of push notifications, amidst broader issues like Iran's internet blackout and increasing cryptocurrency scams. This highlights ongoing challenges in digital security.

item.title: How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors
item.description: The internet's ability to verify authenticity is faltering due to AI-generated content and restricted data access. This crisis affects how people discern truth online, complicating information verification.

item.title: The best AR and MR glasses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: A review of the best AR and MR glasses available in 2026 highlights their ability to blend digital content with real-world experiences, enhancing work and entertainment. This showcases the advancements in immersive technology.

item.title: Hungarian government creds left in the safe hands of 'FrankLampard'
item.description: Hungary's government discovered a significant data breach exposing nearly 800 state logins, including sensitive defense accounts, highlighting vulnerabilities in national security. This incident underscores the importance of strong password practices.

item.title: Enforcing new limits and retiring Opus 4.6 Fast from Copilot Pro+
item.description: GitHub Copilot is implementing new limits and retiring the Opus 4.6 Fast feature due to increased usage patterns. This change aims to manage high concurrency and ensure platform integrity.

item.title: Snowflake manager explains the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI agent data access
item.description: Snowflake's director discussed the 'Spider-Man' theory of AI data access, emphasizing that the quality and governance of data are crucial for developing effective AI agents. This perspective shifts focus from models to data management.

item.title: Here's how to watch the Artemis II splashdown
item.description: NASA's Artemis II mission successfully returned astronauts who traveled farther from Earth than any humans before, marking a significant achievement in space exploration amidst global challenges. This mission reinforces the potential for future space travel.

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now aggregate Copilot cloud agent active user counts
item.description: GitHub has renamed its coding agent to Copilot cloud agent and is updating its data schema to reflect this change. This move aims to streamline user experience and data management.

item.title: Red Hat RHELocates its Chinese engineering team to India
item.description: Red Hat has relocated its Chinese engineering team to India, resulting in significant layoffs, which appears to be influenced by geopolitical factors rather than just downsizing. This shift reflects changing priorities in global operations.

item.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
item.description: Anthropic has temporarily banned the creator of OpenClaw from accessing Claude following a pricing change for OpenClaw users. This decision highlights the challenges of managing user access and pricing in AI services.

item.title: Pausing new GitHub Copilot Pro trials
item.description: In response to abuse of the free trial system, GitHub has paused new trials for Copilot Pro to maintain platform integrity for legitimate users. This decision aims to protect the user experience.

item.title: Actions workflows are limited to 50 reruns
item.description: GitHub has limited Actions workflows to 50 reruns, introducing a cap to manage system performance. Users attempting to exceed this limit will encounter failed checks, impacting workflow efficiency.

item.title: Copilot CLI activity now included in usage metrics totals and feature breakdowns
item.description: GitHub has updated its usage metrics API to include Copilot CLI activity in overall totals, enhancing the feature breakdowns for better insights. This change allows users to track CLI usage alongside other metrics.

item.title: This handy electric screwdriver is now 50% off - here's where to snag the deal
item.description: A 50% discount is available on the Fanttik S1 Pro electric screwdriver, making it an affordable option for DIY enthusiasts and home repair tasks. This deal highlights the growing market for handy electric tools.

item.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
item.description: During National Robotics Week, NVIDIA showcases advancements in physical AI and robotics, emphasizing their impact across various industries like agriculture and manufacturing. These developments are crucial for the future of automated systems.

item.title: This Ryobi yard essentials bundle packs a free power tool - how to get yours
item.description: Home Depot is offering a Ryobi yard essentials bundle that includes a free power tool, encouraging customers to prepare their outdoor spaces. This promotion is part of a broader trend in seasonal home improvement sales.

item.title: 'Finally, a boomless headset for the office': How Jabra made headphones I'd take to a boardroom
item.description: Jabra has released the Evolve3 75, a hybrid headset designed for business use, featuring a sleek design and effective noise cancellation. This product aims to enhance productivity in office environments.

item.title: I used this EcoFlow battery to run my 3,000-sq-ft home in a blackout - here's how it kept my AC on
item.description: The EcoFlow Delta Pro Ultra X battery can power a 3,000-sq-ft home during blackouts, maintaining normal operations like air conditioning. This highlights the importance of reliable backup power solutions.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: Anthropic's new AI model, Mythos, is raising concerns as a potential tool for cybercriminals, prompting a reassessment of security practices among developers. Experts warn that security must be prioritized in AI development.

item.title: Microsoft's Windows Insider Program is no longer a confusing mess
item.description: Microsoft has revamped its Windows Insider Program, aiming to clarify the preview process for Windows 11 users. This update is expected to improve user experience and reduce confusion.

item.title: Forget Shokz: I tried the Suunto Spark earbuds for a month, and they've sold me on air conduction
item.description: Suunto's new air-conduction earbuds have impressed users, outperforming bone-conduction models in sound quality during workouts. This innovation reflects a shift in consumer preferences for audio technology.

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot strategy is just more user abuse from Redmond, says Mozilla
item.description: Mozilla criticizes Microsoft for its Copilot strategy, claiming it compromises user choice in favor of aggressive AI integration. This backlash highlights ongoing tensions in the tech industry regarding user rights.

item.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
item.description: A stalking victim has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, claiming that ChatGPT ignored warnings about a dangerous user, exacerbating her situation. This case raises questions about AI's responsibility in user safety.

item.title: TechCrunch is heading to Tokyo — and bringing the Startup Battlefield with it
item.description: TechCrunch is bringing its Startup Battlefield to Tokyo, focusing on AI, robotics, resilience, and entertainment. The event will showcase innovations and discussions on how these technologies are transforming society.

item.title: Suspect Arrested for Allegedly Throwing Molotov Cocktail at Sam Altman’s Home
item.description: A 20-year-old man has been arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's home, following threats made outside the company's headquarters. The incident raises security concerns for tech leaders.

item.title: 20-year-old man arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman’s house
item.description: San Francisco police arrested a suspect for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at Sam Altman's residence. Surveillance footage captured the incident, which has heightened concerns over safety in the tech community.

item.title: This Startup Wants You to Pay Up to Talk With AI Versions of Human Experts
item.description: Onix is launching a platform where users can pay to interact with AI versions of health and wellness influencers. This service aims to provide personalized advice while promoting products.

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI for Beginners: Getting started with GitHub Copilot CLI
item.description: A tutorial has been released for beginners on how to use GitHub Copilot CLI, providing step-by-step guidance for new users. This aims to enhance user experience with GitHub's coding tools.

item.title: Electronics industry says FCC's foreign-made router policy is a bit of a mesh
item.description: The Global Electronics Association warns that the FCC's ban on foreign-made routers could limit consumer choices and delay access to advanced technology. This policy may hinder Wi-Fi 7 adoption.

item.title: Copilot cloud agent’s validation tools are now 20% faster
item.description: GitHub's Copilot cloud agent has improved its validation tools, making them 20% faster. This enhancement aims to streamline code quality checks for developers.

item.title: Project Glasswing and open source software: The good, the bad, and the ugly
item.description: Anthropic's Project Glasswing aims to address vulnerabilities in open source software using AI. The initiative has raised concerns about AI-generated vulnerabilities while committing $100 million to improve security.

item.title: From 'BuddhaBot' to $1.99 chats with AI Jesus, the faith-based tech boom is here
item.description: The faith-based AI market is growing, with new tools emerging for various religions, including AI versions of spiritual figures. This trend reflects the intersection of technology and spirituality.

item.title: Marketers on the Move: Hires and Exits at Babylist, OpenAI, McDonald’s, and more
item.description: This article highlights recent leadership changes at various companies, including Babylist, OpenAI, and McDonald’s, showcasing the dynamic nature of brand management. These appointments can significantly impact company strategies and market positioning.

item.title: leashd 0.14.0
item.description: Leashd 0.14.0 introduces a safety-first coding framework that allows for agentic coding with pluggable runtimes, enhancing governance in AI applications. This framework supports human oversight, ensuring safer deployment of AI models.

item.title: stormlog 0.3.1
item.description: Stormlog 0.3.1 is a new GPU memory profiler designed for PyTorch and TensorFlow, offering command-line interface, visualization, and analytics features. This tool aims to help developers optimize their deep learning models more effectively.

item.title: claude-code-llm-router 3.4.0
item.description: Claude-code-llm-router 3.4.0 is a multi-LLM router server that enhances complexity routing and integrates with various providers. This update aims to improve subscription monitoring and streamline AI model management.

item.title: LiteParse : Open-Source Tool Finally Fixing OCR’s Biggest Table & Layout Flaws
item.description: LiteParse is an open-source tool developed by Llama Index that addresses significant challenges in OCR, particularly in parsing complex documents. It focuses on structured data extraction while maintaining spatial formatting, improving usability.

item.title: slowai added to PyPI
item.description: Slowai, now available on PyPI, provides a single command to diagnose and fix performance issues in PyTorch models. This tool aims to streamline the debugging process for developers.

item.title: Dew Drop – April 10, 2026 (#4644)
item.description: Dew Drop – April 10, 2026, features a compilation of links addressing various technical issues and updates in the .NET ecosystem. It serves as a resource for developers seeking solutions and insights.

item.title: ramjetio 0.6.5
item.description: Ramjetio 0.6.5 is a distributed cache system designed to enhance PyTorch training efficiency. This update aims to improve the performance of deep learning applications by optimizing data access.

item.title: Is XRP Safer Than Bitcoin? This Analyst Explains The Real Quantum Risk For Holders
item.description: Experts discuss the comparative safety of XRP versus Bitcoin in the context of potential quantum attacks, suggesting XRP's design may offer better protection. This analysis comes amid rising concerns about quantum risks in cryptocurrency.

item.title: Tata Power Collaborates with Databricks to Build Future-Ready Data and AI Platform to Accelerate Energy Transition
item.description: Tata Power is collaborating with Databricks to develop a data and AI platform aimed at accelerating the energy transition. This partnership highlights the growing importance of AI in the energy sector.
